---

# AI 工具生态周报 (2026-W14)

## 1. 本周要闻

- **3月25日**：Anthropic 发布 Claude Code "自动模式"，通过分类器智能跳过权限确认，解决"批准疲劳"问题
- **3月26日**：OpenClaw v2026.3.24 正式发布，新增 OpenAI Realtime API 支持，实现低延迟语音对话
- **3月27日**：字节跳动开源 SuperAgent 框架 Deer-Flow，集成沙箱、记忆、工具链等模块，支持复杂任务处理
- **3月28日**：Mozilla 推出 CQ - Stack Overflow for AI coding agents，构建 Agent 问答基础设施
- **3月29日**：SakanaAI 发布 AI Scientist-v2，基于 agentic tree search 实现 Workshop 级科学发现系统

## 2. CLI 工具进展

**Claude Code**：v2.1.85 发布，优化会话追踪与 VCS 排除功能；重点改进权限控制机制，引入自动模式减少用户交互负担

**OpenAI Codex**：Rust 版本 v0.117.0-alpha.19 持续推进架构重构，重点优化 LSP 支持和执行审批流程

**Gemini CLI**：v0.36.0-preview.6 发布，增强状态监控与错误恢复能力，改善子代理任务追踪体验

**Kimi Code CLI**：v1.27.0 发布，新增 Web 文件面板与安全分析功能，插件系统集成取得突破

**Qwen Code**：v0.14.0-preview.1 发布，深化 VSCode 集成，改进 Plan Mode 操作体验

## 3. AI Agent 生态

**OpenClaw**：
- 发布 v2026.3.24 正式版，新增 OpenAI Realtime API 支持
- 修复 Discord WebSocket 崩溃循环问题，提升网关稳定性
- 推进 Linux GTK4 原生伴侣应用开发，响应跨平台需求
- 引入 `sessions_await` 工具支持并行子代理管理

**生态动态**：
- 多模态 Agent 框架快速崛起，字节 Deer-Flow、Ruvnet Ruflo 等获广泛关注
- Agent 技能插件生态繁荣发展，从信息搜集到代码辅助形成完整工具链
- 具身智能探索活跃，trycua/cua 支持全平台桌面控制

## 4. 开源趋势

**核心方向**：
- **Agent 协作框架**：字节 Deer-Flow、TauricResearch TradingAgents 等多智能体系统受关注
- **垂直场景落地**：金融交易 Agent、渗透测试系统 pentagi 等专业化应用涌现
- **基础能力提升**：浏览器自动化 browser-use、本地模型部署 ollama 等基础设施持续优化
- **RAG 增强**：infiniflow/ragflow、qdrant 等向量数据库与检索技术快速发展

**热点项目**：
- Ollama：支持 Kimi-K2.5、DeepSeek 等前沿模型一键部署
- Dify：生产级 Agentic 工作流平台，集成 RAG 与工具调用
- Mem0：开源 Agent 记忆层解决方案，解决长期上下文保持难题

## 5. HN 社区热议

**核心话题**：
- **Claude Code 使用方法论**：开发者分享高效工作流与思维模式，讨论 AI 编程助手的实际价值边界
- **AI 工具可靠性争议**：Rust 社区反思 AI 辅助编程的工程实践，强调人类审查的不可替代性
- **企业 AI 策略转向**：Walmart 弃用 OpenAI 事件引发对成本效益与定制化需求的深度讨论
- **具身智能前景**：Claude 控制 Mac 设备能力展示，探讨 AI 代理操作物理世界的可能性

**社区情绪**：总体呈现务实倾向，既认可 AI 工具带来的效率提升，也持续关注可靠性、可控性与伦理风险。

## 6. 官方动态

**Anthropic**：
- 发布 Project Fetch 研究：Claude 显著提升机器狗编程效率，验证 AI 在物理世界交互中的赋能作用
- 启动 Science Blog：正式推出 AI×科学交叉领域内容阵地，加速科学研究范式革新
- 与 Mozilla 合作成果：Clau

items.title: 📅 AI 工具生态周报 2026-W14
items.content:
# AI 工具生态周报 2026-W14

> 覆盖日期: 2026-03-24 ~ 2026-03-30 | 生成时间: 2026-03-30 01:31 UTC

---

# AI 工具生态周报 | 2026-W14
**2026-03-24 ~ 2026-03-30**

---

## 1. 本周要闻

| 日期 | 事件 | 核心影响 |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 03-25 | **OpenAI 关闭 Sora 视频平台**，迪士尼取消 10 亿美元投资 | OpenAI 战略收缩标志性事件，社区质疑产品策略稳定性 |
| 03-26 | **Anthropic 发布 Claude Mythos/Capybara 泄露情报**，官方确认"Step change in capabilities" | 下一代模型能力跃迁预期升温，Agent 架构或重构 |
| 03-26 | **Anthropic 发布 Project Vend/Fetch 研究成果**，Claude 实体机器人任务效率提升 2 倍 | "数字-物理世界桥梁"战略落地，AI 科研基础设施定位确立 |
| 03-27 | **Claude Code Auto Mode 正式发布**，基于分类器的权限自动化 | 代理系统从"辅助工具"向"自主执行"跃迁的关键基础设施 |
| 03-28 | **Claude Code 致命 Bug 曝光**：`git reset --hard` 每 10 分钟自动执行 | 开发者对 AI 工具安全设计的信任危机 |
| 03-29 | **OpenClaw v2026.3.28 发布**，废弃 Qwen Portal OAuth，引发认证迁移潮 | 阿里云生态战略调整波及下游工具链 |
| 03-30 | **Claude Code Max 订阅"幽灵限流"危机**，#29579 获 117 评论声讨 | 付费用户信任崩塌，计费透明度成行业共性痛点 |

---

## 2. CLI 工具进展

### 头部工具：稳定性危机与信任重建

| 工具 | 本周状态 | 关键动态 |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Claude Code** | 🔴 **危机模式** | v2.1.83-87 密集补丁链修复 Plan Mode 回归、Git 自动重置 Bug、Max 配额异常；MCP 多服务器支持上线但 OAuth 作用域调整引发断裂 |
| **OpenAI Codex** | 🟡 **高压迭代** | Rust 核心重构加速，5 个 alpha 版本连发；Token 燃烧争议 #14593 达 319 评论，企业级焦虑蔓延；子代理运行时架构密集迭代 |
| **Gemini CLI** | 🟢 **差异化推进** | SDD（Spec-Driven Development）工作流主线推进，v0.36.0-preview 系列持续迭代；实时语音交互 #24174 受关注；内存优化治理 280MB+ 堆膨胀 |
| **GitHub Copilot CLI** | 🟡 **封闭承压** | MCP 组织策略误报、速率限制问题集中爆发；零社区 PR 更新，功能迭代完全依赖官方 Release |
| **Kimi Code CLI** | 🟢 **快速追赶** | v1.25.0-27.0 三连发，插件系统正式 Beta 化；ACP 认证重构、Windows 补齐、大仓库性能优化并行推进 |
| **OpenCode** | 🔴 **外部冲击** | Anthropic OAuth 合规危机触发 Poe 替代方案涌现；Effect 架构迁移攻坚，20+ 贡献者协同修复内存泄漏 |
| **Qwen Code** | 🟢 **高频迭代** | v0.13.0-0.14.0 密集发布，IDE 集成稳定性攻坚；阿里云生态深度整合，Telegram 集成与循环任务功能并进 |

### 共性技术债务
- **成本控制与透明度**：全工具链面临 Token 计费信任危机
- **MCP 生态成熟化**：OAuth 认证流、工具审批粒度、生命周期管理成瓶颈
- **Windows 体验平权**：系统性技术债，全部 7 款工具均有相关高热度 Issue

---

## 3. AI Agent 生态

### OpenClaw 核心动态

| 维度 | 本周进展 |
|:---|:---|
| **版本节奏** | v2026.3.22-3.28 密集发布，含 2 个破坏性变更（Qwen OAuth 废弃、插件安装源优先级调整） |
| **稳定性危机** | v2026.3.22 引入 Control UI 资产缺

items.author: haoruilee

items.title: Formalize human-signing workflow and harden unsigned-tx policies in docs
items.content:
### Motivation
- Enforce a strict human-reviewed, Ledger-only signing boundary and prevent any worker-side signing or broadcasting.
- Provide deterministic control-plane rules and hardened checks so unsigned transaction packages are safe to review and sign.
- Consolidate operational pipeline and review responsibilities to reduce ambiguity during MetaMask + Ledger handoff.

### Description
- Fully rewritten `instructions_final.md` to be the authoritative operations document and added a strict unsigned package schema (`schema: "emperor-os/unsigned-tx/v1"`) and required fields.
- Introduced `signing_manifest.json` as the canonical review object and mandatory ABI-decode, simulation, freshness, allowlist, gas sanity, IPFS verification, nonce, and idempotency checks before any signing.
- Added explicit failure classification states and deterministic runtime rejection rules for MCP write-call responses that include any sign/send semantics.
- Trimmed and aligned `metamask_ledger_handoff.md` to focus on human handoff, the canonical review sequence, and MetaMask + Ledger execution steps that mirror the new checks in `instructions_final.md`.

### Testing
- No automated tests were modified or run because this change is documentation and policy-only; no runtime code changes were included.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69c9c9d853b08330983210f2c9cab141)

items.title: [Regression] Canvas/Node browser tools broken on macOS after v2026.3.24 → v2026.3.28
items.content:
## Describe the bug

After upgrading to OpenClaw v2026.3.28, Cron jobs using Canvas/Browser tools fail. Rolling back to v2026.3.24 restores normal functionality.
## To Reproduce

1. Install OpenClaw v2026.3.28
2. Configure a Cron job that uses Canvas tool to access web pages
3. Execute the Cron job
4. See error: `node command not allowed: the node (platform: darwin) does not support "canvas.navigate"`

## Expected behavior

Canvas tool should support `canvas.navigate` command on macOS Node, as it did in v2026.3.24.

## Actual behavior (v2026.3.28)

- Node pairing successful: `Known: 1 · Paired: 1 · Connected: 1`
- Node capabilities reported: `browser, system`
- But only supports commands: `browser.proxy`, `system.run`, `system.run.prepare`, `system.which`
- **Does NOT support**: `canvas.navigate` (worked in 3.24)

## Environment

- **OS**: macOS (Darwin 24.6.0 arm64)
- **Broken version**: v2026.3.28 (f9b1079)
- **Working version**: v2026.3.24
- **Node**: v25.8.2
- **Gateway**: Running on 127.0.0.1:18789

## Timeline

- **Last week**: v2026.3.24, Cron jobs working correctly ✅
- **Mar 29, 21:08**: Auto-updated to v2026.3.28
- **Mar 30, 10:00+**: Cron jobs started failing ❌
- **Mar 30, 11:50**: Rolled back to v2026.3.24, restored functionality ✅

## Potential Root Causes

v2026.3.28 introduced the following changes that may affect tool execution:

1. **Plugins/hooks**: Added `before_tool_call` hook with `requireApproval`
   - PR: #55339
   - May add extra approval layer before tool execution

2. **Plugins/startup**: Auto-load bundled plugins
   - May change tool initialization order
3. **ACP/channels**: Added Discord current-conversation binds
   - May affect session routing

## Error Logs
## Comparison Test Results

| Command | v2026.3.24 | v2026.3.28 |
|---------|------------|------------|
| `openclaw nodes status` | ✅ Known: 1, Paired: 1 | ✅ Known: 1, Paired: 1 |
| `canvas.navigate` | ✅ Supported | ❌ Not supported |
| `browser.proxy` | ✅ Available | ✅ Available |
|

items.title: chore: update dependencies and refactor imports for openclaw plugin-sdk
items.content:
14:52:47+08:00 [plugins] openclaw-seatalk failed during register from 
openclaw-seatalk/index.ts: TypeError: (0 , _pluginSdk.normalizeAccountId) is not a function

问题定位清楚了。openclaw-seatalk 插件在 src/accounts.ts 第 2 行从 "openclaw/plugin-sdk" 导入了 normalizeAccountId 和 DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID：

accounts.ts
Lines 2-2
import { DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID, normalizeAccountId } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk";
但当前版本的 OpenClaw (2026.3.24) 的 openclaw/plugin-sdk 主入口 (src/plugin-sdk/index.ts) 没有导出这两个符号。它们存在于子路径模块中。

问题根因找到了。

插件的 devDependencies 装的是 openclaw@2026.2.9，那个版本的 openclaw/plugin-sdk 主入口（compat barrel）导出了 normalizeAccountId。但你本地 OpenClaw 是 2026.3.24，主入口已经不导出这个符号了 — 它被拆到了 openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id 子路径。

因为插件用 --link 安装，运行时 openclaw/plugin-sdk 解析到的是本地 OpenClaw 的 src/plugin-sdk/index.ts（通过 jiti alias），而不是插件自己 node_modules 里的旧版本。


This pull request updates dependencies and refines import paths for better modularity and compatibility with the latest `openclaw` package. The primary focus is on updating the `openclaw` dependency and refactoring imports to use more specific module paths, which helps improve code maintainability and reduces the risk of breaking changes from upstream updates.

Dependency updates:

* Updated `openclaw` to version `2026.3.24` and `@types/ws` to `^8.18.0` in `package.json`, ensuring compatibility with recent changes and improvements in these packages.

Import path refactoring:

* Changed imports of `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID` and related utilities to reference specific submodules (e.g., `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id` and `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup`) instead of the main `plugin-sdk` entry point in `src/accounts.ts`, `src/channel.ts`, and `src/onboarding.ts`. This modularizes dependencies and aligns with the updated `openclaw` package structure. items.author: hawtim

items.title: Env vars from .env not passed to sandbox exec calls — generic provider placeholder not resolved
items.content:
## Description

Environment variables defined in `~/.openclaw/.env` are not available inside sandbox `exec` calls. When a generic OpenShell provider is configured (e.g., `ncbi` with credential key `NCBI_API_KEY`), the env var inside the sandbox resolves to the OpenShell placeholder `openshell:resolve:env:NCBI_API_KEY` rather than the actual value.

It's unclear whether OpenClaw is responsible for passing the resolved env var value to OpenShell when creating the sandbox, or whether OpenShell's L7 proxy should resolve the placeholder. Filing here for the OpenClaw side of the issue.

## Setup

**openclaw.json** (relevant sections):
```json
{
  "env": {
    "shellEnv": {
      "enabled": true,
      "timeoutMs": 15000
    }
  },
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "openshell": {
        "enabled": true,
        "config": {
          "from": "openshell/sandbox-from:1774813410",
          "providers": ["ncbi"]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

**~/.openclaw/.env**:
```
NCBI_API_KEY=<redacted>
```

**OpenShell provider**:
```
openshell provider create --name ncbi --type generic --credential NCBI_API_KEY
```

**Gateway**: running via systemd (user service), which has `Environment=NCBI_API_KEY=<value>` set.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Configure as above
2. In a sandbox exec call, reference `$NCBI_API_KEY` in a curl URL:
   ```
   exec curl -s "https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/esearch.fcgi?db=pubmed&api_key=$NCBI_API_KEY&term=test"
   ```

3. The L7 proxy log shows the URL contains `api_key=openshell:resolve:env:NCBI_API_KEY` instead of the actual key.

## Expected behavior

`$NCBI_API_KEY` should resolve to the real value inside sandbox exec calls — either:
- OpenClaw passes the resolved value to OpenShell when creating the sandbox, or
- OpenClaw instructs OpenShell to resolve the placeholder before the request leaves the proxy

## Actual behavior

The placeholder `openshell:resolve:env:NCBI_API_KEY` is passed through unresolved.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: v

items.title: Track non-memex operational cleanup in local OpenClaw environment
items.content:
## Context

These are not memex code regressions, but they keep showing up during memex validation and should be tracked explicitly.

## Current local issues

- embedded agent WebSocket streaming falls back to HTTP (`Unexpected server response: 500`)
- `safeBinTrustedDirs` warnings for group-writable dirs
- stale `memex-audit-*` sessions remain in OpenClaw status output
- memex store still reports some noise entries worth purging

## Scope

Track and clean these up separately from core memex retrieval/benchmark work so operational noise does not get conflated with plugin quality regressions.

items.title: feat: multi-platform plugin support — Codex, OpenClaw, OpenCode
items.content:
## Summary

- Decouple platform-specific transcript parsing from core library
- Migrate `ccplugin/` → `plugins/claude-code/` for consistent plugin directory structure
- Add **Codex CLI** plugin (`plugins/codex/`): shell hooks + SKILL.md, LLM summarization
- Add **OpenClaw** plugin (`plugins/openclaw/`): TypeScript, registerTool, per-agent memory isolation
- Add **OpenCode** plugin (`plugins/opencode/`): TypeScript npm plugin, SQLite capture daemon
- Improve ONNX embedding offline cache for sandboxed environments
- Fix Milvus Lite server lock leak via `release_server()` on close
- Restructure documentation: platform plugins as top-level tabs, Home sub-pages

## Platforms

| Platform | Plugin Type | Capture | Recall | Install |
|----------|-------------|---------|--------|---------|
| Claude Code | Shell hooks + SKILL.md | Stop hook → haiku summary | 3-layer skill (fork) | Marketplace |
| Codex CLI | Shell hooks + SKILL.md | codex exec (isolated) | 3-layer skill | install.sh |
| OpenClaw | TS registerTool | llm_output debounce | memory_search/get/transcript tools | openclaw plugins install |
| OpenCode | TS npm plugin | SQLite polling daemon | memory_search/get/transcript tools | opencode.json |

## Test plan

- [x] pytest: 77 passed
- [x] Claude Code: interactive TUI with --plugin-dir (SessionStart, skill L1/L2, Stop hook)
- [x] Codex: interactive TUI with --yolo (SessionStart, $memory-recall, Stop hook LLM summary)
- [x] OpenClaw: interactive TUI on EC2 (plugin loaded, memory_search/get/transcript, afterTurn capture, per-agent isolation)
- [x] OpenCode: TUI + opencode run (memory_search/get/transcript, capture daemon, LLM summary, multi-turn, multi-directory)
- [x] Cross-platform: markdown memory format and anchors consistent across all platforms

items.author: dependabot[bot]

items.title: fix(plugins): preserve gateway subagent binding during memory bootstrap
items.content:
## Summary
Preserve gateway subagent binding when the memory runtime bootstraps plugins.

## Root cause
OpenClaw's gateway startup installs a gateway-bindable plugin runtime, but `src/plugins/memory-runtime.ts` later re-resolved plugins with a plain `resolveRuntimePluginRegistry({ config })` call.

On deployments with memory enabled, that path could replace the active plugin runtime with one that no longer allowed gateway subagent binding. Plugin `before_tool_call` hooks would then fail with:

`Plugin runtime subagent methods are only available during a gateway request.`

In our case this showed up in `openclaw-scanner`: subagent review failed first, then its HTTP fallback needed a stronger token scope.

## What changes
- pass `runtimeOptions.allowGatewaySubagentBinding = true` when the memory runtime resolves plugins
- update the memory runtime test to lock in that contract

## Why this is the right fix
The bug is not in plugin review logic. It is the memory bootstrap path downgrading the plugin runtime after gateway startup. Keeping gateway subagent binding on that path preserves the runtime surface that gateway-backed hooks expect.

## Testing
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts src/plugins/memory-runtime.test.ts`
items.author: ninjaa

items.title: feat: universal model compatibility filter and thought signature cache
items.content:
## Summary

- **Universal non-chat model filter**: Centralized regex filter in `ProviderModelFetcherService.fetch()` applied to ALL providers after parsing. Catches TTS, embedding, image generation, speech recognition, and other non-chat models. Provider-specific patterns supplement the universal filter (OpenAI: moderation/davinci/sora/audio, Gemini: aqs/nano-banana, Mistral: OCR).
- **Tool support filter**: Models where models.dev confirms `toolCall: false` are filtered at discovery time. In the OpenClaw context, every request includes tools — models without tool calling will always fail.
- **Tool-aware tier assignment**: `TierAutoAssignService.pickBest()` now prefers tool-capable models for standard, complex, and reasoning tiers.
- **Relaxed modality check**: Output modality check changed from "text-only" to "includes text", allowing multimodal models that can produce text (e.g., GPT-4o) to pass through.
- **Thought signature cache**: New `ThoughtSignatureCache` stores `thought_signature` values from Google Gemini thinking model responses and re-injects them when clients strip them during round-trips, preventing 400 errors with newer Gemini models.

## Test plan

- [x] All 3178 backend unit tests pass
- [x] New test file `filter-non-chat-models.spec.ts` covers universal and provider-specific filters (36 tests)
- [x] New test file `thought-signature-cache.spec.ts` covers store/retrieve/clear/expiry (10 tests)
- [x] Updated existing tests for relaxed modality checks and tool support filter
- [ ] CI passes
- [ ] Manual: Connect Google API key → verify TTS/embedding/Imagen models not shown
- [ ] Manual: Verify standard Gemini models (2.5 Flash, 2.5 Pro) still appear

items.title: 🐛 fix(message-bridge): correct assistant map env and create session directory logs
items.content:
# PR 说明

## 变更摘要

修复 `message-bridge` 中 assistant 目录映射环境变量的错误拼写，并将 `action.create_session.started` 调整为记录最终用于创建会话的目录与来源。

## 为什么要改

当前仓库仍使用错误拼写的 `BRIDGE_ASSIANT_DIRECTORY_MAP_FILE`，会把错误配置名继续外溢到联调和文档侧。

同时，`action.create_session.started` 之前打印的是中间态目录，无法从日志直接确认最终是以哪个目录创建的会话，影响联调排障。

## 影响范围 / 受影响工作区

- [x] `plugins/message-bridge`
- [ ] `plugins/message-bridge-openclaw`
- [ ] `packages/test-support`
- [ ] `integration/opencode-cui`
- [ ] `.github`
- [x] `docs`
- [ ] 其他（请补充）

## 验证证据

请列出实际执行过的命令和结果摘要；如果没有执行，也必须说明原因。

```bash
pnpm --dir plugins/message-bridge run typecheck
pnpm --dir plugins/message-bridge run test:unit
pnpm --dir plugins/message-bridge run test:integration
pnpm verify:workspace
```

结果摘要：

- `message-bridge` typecheck 通过
- `message-bridge` unit tests 通过
- `message-bridge` integration tests 通过
- workspace 级 build/test/verify 全部通过

未执行项与原因：

- 无

## 行为 / 契约验证

请明确说明这次改动影响了哪些行为、边界或契约，以及它们分别如何被验证。

- 受影响行为 / 契约：`BRIDGE_ASSISTANT_DIRECTORY_MAP_FILE` 成为唯一有效的目录映射文件环境变量入口
- 对应验证方式：更新 config snapshot、unit/integration tests 中的环境变量设置与断言
- 验证结果摘要：仓库内相关代码、测试和文档均已切换到正确拼写，旧拼写不再残留

- 受影响行为 / 契约：`action.create_session.started` 记录最终目录而非中间态目录
- 对应验证方式：补充 `CreateSessionAction` 单测，覆盖 `mapping` 与 `none` 两种来源；集成测试继续覆盖 `uniassistant` 映射和 fallback 链路
- 验证结果摘要：日志现在输出 `resolvedDirectory` 与 `resolvedDirectorySource`，可直接判断最终创建目录

## 风险与回滚关注点

主要风险是本地联调脚本或环境文件如果仍使用旧变量名，将不再生效。日志字段从 `effectiveDirectory` 切换为 `resolvedDirectory` / `resolvedDirectorySource`，依赖旧字段名的外部检索脚本需要同步调整。

如果需要回滚，可直接回退本 PR 恢复旧环境变量名与原有日志字段。

## 关联 Issue

- Closes #39

## 提交前检查

- [x] 我已确认本仓库默认目标分支是 `main`
- [x] 我已使用仓库模板补全必要信息
- [x] 我已明确列出受影响工作区
- [x] 我已提供验证证据，或明确说明未执行原因
- [x] 我已说明受影响行为 / 契约及其验证方式
- [x] 我已避免把无关工作树变更混入这次 PR

items.author: mylukin

items.title: Generic provider env vars not resolved in URL query parameters by L7 proxy
items.content:
## Description

When using a `generic` provider with a credential key (e.g., `NCBI_API_KEY`), the env var inside the sandbox resolves to the literal placeholder string `openshell:resolve:env:NCBI_API_KEY` instead of the actual credential value. The L7 proxy reports `credentials_injected: true` but does not substitute the placeholder in URL query parameters.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Create a generic provider with a credential key:
   ```
   openshell provider create --name ncbi --type generic --credential NCBI_API_KEY
   ```

2. Inside a sandbox, run a curl command using the env var in a URL query parameter:
   ```
   curl -s "https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/esearch.fcgi?db=pubmed&retmode=json&api_key=$NCBI_API_KEY&term=test"
   ```

3. The L7 proxy log shows:
   ```
   credentials_injected: true
   host: eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov
   path: /entrez/eutils/esearch.fcgi?db=pubmed&retmode=json&api_key=openshell:resolve:env:NCBI_API_KEY&term=test
   ```

## Expected behavior

`$NCBI_API_KEY` should resolve to the actual credential value inside the sandbox shell, or the L7 proxy should substitute `openshell:resolve:env:NCBI_API_KEY` with the real value before forwarding the request.

## Actual behavior

- The sandbox env var contains the literal string `openshell:resolve:env:NCBI_API_KEY`
- Shell expansion substitutes this placeholder into the URL
- The L7 proxy claims `credentials_injected: true` but the placeholder remains in the outbound URL query parameter
- The upstream API receives the placeholder string instead of the key, causing auth failures

## Environment

- OpenShell version: 0.0.16
- Provider type: generic
- Running via OpenClaw gateway (systemd service)
- Sandbox backend: openshell

## Workaround

Hardcode the API key directly in the curl URL instead of using the env var.

items.author: GoodspeedWong

items.title: telegram: improve polling outage detection and recovery after network loss
items.content:
## Background

In real-world network interruption tests, the current Telegram polling recovery path is not sensitive enough to connection loss.

After repeated observation on a local OpenClaw deployment, we saw cases where Telegram connectivity had already been broken for a long time, but the process did not emit a meaningful error log until much later. In some runs, the gap was well over 15 minutes before a clear failure signal appeared in the logs.

This PR adjusts Telegram polling supervision to make outage detection and recovery more responsive under real network interruptions.

## What changed

### 1. Heartbeat supervisor (outer loop)

A heartbeat supervisor runs at the `runUntilAbort()` level — above individual polling cycles — so it persists across cycle restarts and controls the polling lifecycle:

- Periodically probes Telegram with `getMe` requests through the same transport as `getUpdates` (respects `forceIpv4`, DNS tuning, proxy config, sticky IPv4 fallback).
- API base is resolved once from the account's `apiRoot` config and passed via `opts.apiBase`, so deployments using a custom Telegram Bot API server are correctly handled.
- Tracks consecutive heartbeat failures.
- After reaching the failure threshold (3 consecutive failures):
  - aborts the current polling cycle via a cycle-scoped `AbortController`
  - marks transport dirty for rebuild on next cycle
  - enters a quiet wait state and keeps probing without restart loops
- Once heartbeat recovers, releases the gate so the main loop starts a new polling cycle.

Implementation details that matter operationally:
- Heartbeat uses the polling transport's fetch (`TelegramPollingTransportState.currentTransport()`), so it follows the same network path as polling.
- Heartbeat response bodies are explicitly canceled so sockets are returned to the connection pool.
- The outer heartbeat loop has its own stop controller so fatal errors can still propagate cleanly; `runUntilAbort()` does not hang in `finally`.

###
items.author: sinogello

items.title: chore: unify desktop dev launch under scripts/dev
items.content:
## What

Unify desktop local-dev launch under `scripts/dev`, absorb the old mac shell launch responsibilities into platform helpers, and document the mac validation plan plus the known `pnpm test`/dev-openclaw interference FAQ.

## Why

The desktop dev path had drifted across `scripts/dev` and legacy `dev-*.sh` shell scripts, which caused mac-specific launch issues, stale status reporting, and unreliable stop/restart behavior. We need `scripts/dev` to be the single entry while preserving the existing mac compatibility behaviors.

## How

- move desktop launch compatibility into `scripts/dev/src/shared/platform/desktop-dev-platform.*` with a platform-agnostic entry and darwin/win32 implementations
- switch desktop dev to an internal composite model: explicit Vite worker + explicit Electron main, while keeping the external `desktop` target unchanged
- disable implicit Electron startup from desktop Vite under `scripts/dev` and track both Electron/Vite PIDs for status/stop/restart
- fix mac dev port detection in `dev-utils` and update openclaw snapshot handling so internal restarts do not report stale incorrectly
- remove obsolete `apps/desktop/scripts/dev-run.sh` and `dev-env.sh`, then update invariants/docs to point at `scripts/dev`
- add `TASK.mac.md` to capture the mac validation plan and the known FAQ that full `pnpm test` can kill the live dev openclaw process

## Affected areas

- [x] Desktop app (Electron shell)
- [x] Controller (backend / API)
- [ ] Web dashboard (React UI)
- [ ] OpenClaw runtime
- [ ] Skills
- [ ] Shared schemas / packages
- [x] Build / CI / Tooling

## Checklist

- [x] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [x] `pnpm lint` passes
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes
- [ ] `pnpm generate-types` run (if API routes/schemas changed)
- [x] No credentials or tokens in code or logs
- [x] No `any` types introduced (use `unknown` with narrowing)

## Notes for reviewers

- The main review focus is the new desktop dev ownership model in `scripts/dev`: explicit Vite + explicit E

items.author: sinogello

items.title: fix: allow absolute paths for OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH and OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR in workspace .env
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes a regression introduced in 2026.3.28 where `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` were blocked from being loaded from a project-local `.env` file, even when set to absolute paths.

## Root Cause

PR #54631 added both keys to `BLOCKED_WORKSPACE_DOTENV_KEYS` to prevent malicious workspace files from redirecting config/state loading (e.g. `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=./evil-config.json`). However, this was overly broad and also blocked the legitimate user case of configuring these paths to absolute locations in a project `.env`.

## Fix

The security concern is specifically about **relative paths**, not absolute paths. Absolute paths are safe because they are explicitly configured by the user — they cannot redirect loading to an attacker-controlled relative location.

This PR introduces `ABSOLUTE_PATH_ONLY_WORKSPACE_DOTENV_KEYS` — a set of keys that are allowed from workspace `.env` only when the value is an absolute path. Relative path values for these keys remain blocked.

## Changes

- `src/infra/dotenv.ts`: Replace broad block on `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` with a value-aware check (`path.isAbsolute`) that only blocks relative paths
- `src/infra/dotenv.test.ts`: Add tests for both allowed (absolute path) and blocked (relative path) cases

## Testing

All existing tests pass. New tests added and verified:
- ✅ Allows absolute `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` from workspace `.env`
- ✅ Blocks relative `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` from CWD `.env`
- ✅ Blocks relative path values even when env vars are unset

Fixes #57408

items.title: [Skill] add prd-to-app and web-replication skill
items.content:
# Contribution Guide

We welcome your contributions to this repository. To ensure elegant code style and better code quality, we have prepared
the following contribution guidelines.

## What We Accept

+ This PR fixes a typo or improves the documentation (if this is the case, you may skip the other checks).
+ This PR fixes a specific issue — please reference the issue number in the PR description. Make sure your code strictly
  follows the coding standards below.
+ This PR introduces a new feature — please clearly explain the necessity and implementation of the feature. Make sure
  your code strictly follows the coding standards below.

## Code Style Guide

Good code style is an art. We have prepared a `pre-commit` hook to enforce consistent code
formatting across the project. You can clean up your code following the steps below:

```shell
pre-commit run --all-files
```

If your code complies with the standards, you should not see any errors.

## Naming Conventions

+ Please use **English** for naming; do not use Pinyin or other languages. All comments should also be in English.
+ Follow **PEP8** naming conventions strictly, and use underscores to separate words. Avoid meaningless names such as
  `a`, `b`, `c`.

## For glmv-reward Contributors

Before PR, Please run:

```bash
cd glmv-reward/
uv sync
uv run poe lint
uv run poe typecheck
```
items.author: JaredforReal

items.title: 撰寫：agentskills 研究筆記（zh-TW，可直接複用）
items.content:
## 目標
針對 repo `https://github.com/agentskills/agentskills` 撰寫一篇 **繁體中文（zh-TW）** 的研究筆記，放入 `content/Claude/` 底下，內容要能讓我之後快速複用。

## 產出位置
- 建議檔案：`content/Claude/agentskills-guide.md`
- 若研究後覺得應放在更細的子目錄（例如 `content/Claude/Tools&Skills/`），可自行調整，但請在 issue comment 說明最終路徑。

## 核心要求
這篇不是單純摘要，而是要做成「**下次可以直接拿來用**」的筆記文章。

請至少包含以下章節：

### 1. TL;DR
- 5~10 點重點摘要
- 說清楚這個 repo 值不值得看、適合誰看、什麼情境最有用

### 2. Repo 在講什麼
- 專案定位
- 主要內容範圍
- 與 Claude Code / agent workflow / prompt engineering / tools / workspace 的關係

### 3. 分類整理（Categorised Sections）
請把內容按主題整理，不要流水帳。
至少拆成幾類（可增減）：
- Skill spec / skill anatomy
- Prompt / instruction patterns
- Workflow / usage patterns
- Tooling / integrations
- Workspace / file structure
- Safety / guardrails
- Reusable playbooks / templates

每一類都要寫：
- 這類在 repo 中講了什麼
- 為什麼重要
- 我下次可以怎麼用

### 4. 可直接複用的做法
整理成方便未來直接套用的內容，例如：
- checklist
- prompt/template
- SOP
- decision rules
- do / don't

### 5. 圖表 / 結構圖（Charts）
至少提供 2 種視覺化整理，使用 **Markdown / Mermaid** 即可，例如：
- skill 結構圖
- workflow 流程圖
- decision tree
- 對照表

### 6. 與我目前工作流的連結
請加一節：**How I can use this next time**
要站在實務角度，把 agentskills 的內容映射到我可能會做的事情，例如：
- 建立新 skill
- 整理既有 prompt/workflow 成 skill
- coding task / research task 的 skill 化
- team knowledge packaging
- OpenClaw / Claude Code / agent workflow 類似場景

### 7. 建議保留的速查區
文末請做一個快速參考區（Quick Reference），讓我之後不用重讀整篇。

## 品質要求
- 全文使用 **繁體中文（zh-TW）**
- 結構清楚、標題命名一致
- 不是只有翻譯，要有整理、抽象、歸納
- 優先寫成「可重用知識資產」
- 若 repo 內有值得引用的範例、章節或模式，請保留連結或出處

## Definition of Done
- [ ] 在 `content/Claude/`（或合理子路徑）新增文章
- [ ] 文章為 zh-TW
- [ ] 有分類章節
- [ ] 有至少 2 個圖表 / Mermaid 圖
- [ ] 有 `How I can use this next time` 區塊
- [ ] 有 Quick Reference
- [ ] 在 issue 留 comment：附檔案路徑 + 3~5 行摘要

## 參考來源
- Repo: https://github.com/agentskills/agentskills

items.title: [codex] refresh cluster perf baseline
items.content:
## Summary

I refreshed the cluster performance baseline to match the improved CI measurements from the recent clustering speedup.

I also updated the cluster perf benchmark output so CI reports a copyable `Suggested baseline update` object whenever the current run beats the checked-in baseline, and I documented in `AGENTS.md` that intentional perf wins should update the checked-in baseline right away.

## Why

The checked-in baseline was still using the older `535.1 ms` fixture median and `10m 0.0s` projected `openclaw/openclaw` duration even though the current CI run for this benchmark reported `450.6 ms` and `8m 25.3s`.

That left the perf check showing a large negative delta against stale reference numbers instead of comparing future runs against the current expected performance.

## Validation

I ran:

- `pnpm test:cluster-perf`
- `pnpm --filter @ghcrawl/api-contract build && pnpm --filter @ghcrawl/api-core typecheck`

items.title: perf: add static asset caching headers
items.content:
## Summary

Two performance improvements that make Conductor permanently fast without manual intervention.

**Commit 1: Caching headers**
- Static assets (_next/static/*) get immutable 1-year cache
- API routes get no-store/no-cache for fresh data

**Commit 2: Automatic session cleanup janitor**
- Background task runs on startup and every 6 hours
- Clears recoveryState from terminal sessions older than 24h (prevents recovering_sessions accumulation)
- Removes stale terminal capture/restore files for terminal sessions
- Deletes session JSON files for sessions older than 7 days
- Logs cleanup counts via tracing::info

## User-Facing Release Notes

- Backend stays fast permanently: stale sessions are cleaned up automatically every 6 hours
- No more 33 recovering sessions on startup — the janitor clears them before they accumulate
- Browser caches static assets for 1 year, reducing load times on repeat visits

## Type of Change

- [x] New feature
- [x] Refactor / chore

## Checklist

- [x] `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` passes
- [x] `cargo test -p conductor-server --lib session_store` passes
- [x] Build passes

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Enhanced cache rules: API responses are no-cache/must-revalidate; static assets use long-lived immutable caching.
  * Added an automated, configurable Strix security scan workflow in CI.
  * Launched a background session cleanup job to remove stale terminal/session artifacts.

* **Documentation**
  * Added a security review and Strix setup/instructions documenting scan scope, usage, and mitigation notes.

* **Improvements**
  * More robust streaming/parsing for live feed and SSE handling; dispatcher matching reliability improved.

* **Tests**
  * Added unit tests covering SSE/stream parsing and dispatcher lookup behavior.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

items.author: dependabot[bot]

items.title: Feature Request: Feishu plugin support for card.action.trigger event
items.content:
## Summary

The bundled Feishu plugin currently supports `im.message.receive_v1` events via WebSocket but does **not** handle `card.action.trigger` callback events. When a user clicks an interactive card button (e.g., approve/reject/complete), the event arrives at the WebSocket connection but is silently dropped because no handler is registered.

## Use Case

We use OpenClaw gateway on multiple nodes (skynet-alpha, able-cc) with Feishu bots for team collaboration. Our central PROD server sends interactive task cards to team members via Feishu. When a member clicks "Complete" or "Reject", we need the callback to be processed — either locally by the gateway or forwarded to the central server.

Current workaround: We created a separate dedicated Bot (AetherOS Task Bot) whose WebSocket is held exclusively by PROD, bypassing the per-node gateway entirely. This works but adds unnecessary complexity.

## Expected Behavior

The Feishu plugin should:

1. Register `card.action.trigger` in the EventDispatcherHandler (alongside `im.message.receive_v1`)
2. When a card action event arrives, either:
   - a. Dispatch it to the agent session (like a message), or
   - b. Forward it via a configurable hook/webhook, or
   - c. Write it to a local file (e.g., inbox) for external processing

Option (a) would be ideal — the agent could process the callback and respond.

## Environment

- OpenClaw version: latest (homebrew)
- Feishu plugin: bundled (`dist/plugin-sdk/feishu.js`)
- Connection mode: WebSocket
- OS: macOS (Apple Silicon)

## Evidence

Gateway log shows message events are dispatched but card action events produce no log at all:

```
[feishu] feishu[default]: received message from ou_xxx in oc_xxx (p2p)
[feishu] feishu[default]: dispatching to agent (session=agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_xxx)
[feishu] feishu[default]: dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=1)
// No log for card.action.trigger — event silently dropped
```

The lark-oapi SDK fully supports this event type via

items.author: dependabot[bot]

items.title: [Proposal] 修正 assistant 目录映射环境变量拼写并让 create_session.started 打印最终目录
items.content:
## 背景与问题

当前 `plugins/message-bridge` 中用于 assistant 目录映射文件的环境变量名为 `BRIDGE_ASSIANT_DIRECTORY_MAP_FILE`，其中 `ASSIANT` 存在拼写错误。这个错误拼写已经进入运行时代码、测试和文档，当前文档还明确说明“保留历史拼写”。

在当前仍处于开发联调阶段的前提下，继续保留错误拼写会放大后续接入成本，并把错误命名继续外溢到用户侧配置。

另外，`action.create_session.started` 当前打印的是中间态目录信息，而不是最终实际用于创建会话的目录。当目录经过映射或最终解析后，现有日志无法直接确认会话究竟是以哪个目录创建的，这会影响联调排障。

## 目标

- 将 assistant 目录映射文件环境变量统一修正为正确拼写。
- 刷新相关文档与示例，公开文档中不再出现错误拼写字段。
- 让 `action.create_session.started` 能反映最终实际用于创建会话的目录，便于从日志直接判断创建上下文。

## 建议方案

- 将运行时环境变量入口从 `BRIDGE_ASSIANT_DIRECTORY_MAP_FILE` 修正为 `BRIDGE_ASSISTANT_DIRECTORY_MAP_FILE`。
- 同步更新 `plugins/message-bridge` 内相关测试、环境快照、配置契约文档和示例配置。
- 文档中删除对错误拼写字段的公开说明，不再把它作为推荐或保留入口继续传播。
- 调整 `action.create_session.started` 的打印时机或字段来源，使其记录最终传给 session create 调用的目录，而不是解析前/映射前的中间态目录。
- 同步更新日志参考文档，明确该日志字段表达的是“最终创建目录”。

## Out of scope / 非目标

- 不修改下行协议字段。
- 不调整 `integration/opencode-cui` 夹具或 submodule 指针。
- 不顺带处理与本 issue 无关的其他环境变量清理。

## 预估影响范围

- [x] `plugins/message-bridge`
- [ ] `plugins/message-bridge-openclaw`
- [ ] `packages/test-support`
- [ ] `integration/opencode-cui`
- [ ] `.github` / workflow
- [ ] 发布流程
- [x] 文档需要同步

## 验收标准

- `plugins/message-bridge` 运行时代码使用 `BRIDGE_ASSISTANT_DIRECTORY_MAP_FILE` 作为目录映射文件环境变量入口。
- 仓库内相关测试、fixture 和示例配置已同步到正确拼写。
- `plugins/message-bridge/docs/` 下对外文档不再出现 `BRIDGE_ASSIANT_DIRECTORY_MAP_FILE` 作为公开配置项。
- `action.create_session.started` 日志可以直接看到最终用于创建会话的目录。
- 日志参考文档与实际日志字段保持一致。

## 风险与依赖

- 已有本地联调脚本、环境文件或测试辅助代码如果仍使用错误拼写，需要同步迁移，否则目录映射可能失效。
- 如果 `create_session` 最终目录是在 use case 内部解析得到，日志埋点位置可能需要后移，或改为在解析完成后再记录 started 事件，以避免继续暴露中间态。

items.title: ⚡ Bolt: optimize MMR re-ranking complexity to O(N^2)
items.content:
💡 What: Optimized the MMR (Maximal Marginal Relevance) re-ranking algorithm.
🎯 Why: The previous implementation had $O(N^3)$ complexity, causing significant slowdowns (e.g., ~61.8 seconds for 1000 items) due to redundant similarity re-calculations.
📊 Impact: Reduced complexity to $O(N^2)$, achieving approximately 137x speedup (~451ms for 1000 items).
🔬 Measurement: Verified with a custom benchmark script `openclaw_repo/extensions/memory-core/src/memory/mmr.perf.ts` and existing unit tests.

---

items.title: [Bug] Cron job payload.model override ignored - always uses agent default
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Cron job isolated agentTurn payload.model is ignored. Setting model to dashscope/kimi-k2.5 causes job to always execute with agent default (zai/glm-5-turbo) instead. Tested with multiple jobs and configurations, 100% reproducible.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure dashscope provider with kimi-k2.5 in openclaw.json 2. Create isolated cron: openclaw cron add --name Test --session isolated --model dashscope/kimi-k2.5 3. Verify payload.model in jobs.json 4. Run: openclaw cron run 5. Check: openclaw cron runs 6. Result: model=glm-5-turbo (payload.model ignored)

### Expected behavior

Per docs at /automation/cron-jobs, model resolution priority: 1) Job payload override (highest) 2) Hook-specific defaults 3) Agent config default. payload.model should take precedence. Expected: job executes with dashscope/kimi-k2.5.

### Actual behavior

Job always executes with agent default (zai/glm-5-turbo), ignoring payload.model. Evidence from openclaw cron runs: model=glm-5-turbo, provider=zai. Tried --model via cron edit, --clear-agent, fresh jobs. Same result every time.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.28 (f9b1079)

### Operating system

Windows 10 19045 (x64)

### Install method

npm global install

### Model

dashscope/kimi-k2.5 (payload) / zai/glm-5-turbo (actual)

### Provider / routing chain

dashscope (coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com) in openclaw.json models.providers

### Additional provider/model setup details

dashscope provider config: baseUrl=coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1, api_key auth, models: kimi-k2.5, qwen3.5-plus, MiniMax-M2.5. Direct API test to dashscope returns HTTP 200 with valid response. Model field persists correctly in jobs.json storage.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Run evidence: status=ok model=glm-5-turbo provider=zai. jobs.json stores payload.model=dashscope/kimi-k2.5 correctly. Same across all 3 cron jobs.
```

items.author: wusemao

items.title: Discord plain-text agent messages can be delivered as interactive modal/form payloads
items.content:
## Summary
On Discord, what should be a plain text agent message can end up being delivered as an interactive modal/form payload instead of a normal text message.

I hit this while trying to send a normal supervisory message into a Discord thread. Instead of a plain chat message, Discord rendered an interactive form/modal with a field like `x`.

This appears to be happening in real usage, and not only once.

## Environment
- OpenClaw: `2026.3.28`
- Host: macOS `26.3`, arm64
- Channel: Discord
- Delivery target: Discord thread / guild channel
- Gateway status at reproduction time: healthy

## What I expected
A normal plain text Discord message in the thread.

## What actually happened
Discord received an interactive payload and rendered a modal/form-like UI instead of a normal message.

## Docs consulted
- `docs/channels/discord.md`
- `docs/gateway/configuration.md`
- `docs/automation/cron-jobs.md`

Relevant Discord docs say components/modals should only be present when explicitly provided via a `components` payload.

## Reproduction sketch
1. Use the agent / message tool to send what is intended to be a plain text Discord message.
2. Do not intentionally request buttons, modals, polls, or forms.
3. Observe that Discord sometimes receives an interactive payload anyway.
4. The rendered result is not plain text. It becomes a form/modal style interaction.

## Why this looks like a product bug
In my case, the actual send path behaved as if `components` or `components.modal` were present even though the intended user-visible action was plain text only.

Two possibilities:
1. The Discord adapter or tool bridge is incorrectly materializing empty/default component structures into a real Discord components payload.
2. Validation / serialization treats an empty or placeholder `components` shape as a live modal instead of ignoring it.

## Additional evidence
- Discord docs indicate components are advanced and explicit.
- Plain text sends should not silently become interactive p

items.content:
## Reporter
Mother 🫶 (Harness Review)

## Type
critical

## Priority
critical

## Description
**Widespread cron job failures detected during daily harness review:**

### Failed Jobs (15 total):
- Herald Health Check - timeout, 3 consecutive errors
- Token Usage Tracker - timeout, 11 consecutive errors  
- Agent Harness Version Check - timeout, 12 consecutive errors
- Mother Evening Report - timeout, 2 consecutive errors
- Daily Harness Review - timeout, 2 consecutive errors
- Daily Log Collection (Walter) - timeout, 2 consecutive errors
- SNT AI Doctor (Walter) - timeout, 2 consecutive errors
- OpenClaw Source Update - timeout, 2 consecutive errors
- Mother Morning Routine - timeout, 2 consecutive errors
- Daily Research (Claude Team) - timeout, 2 consecutive errors
- Mother Midday Report - timeout, 2 consecutive errors
- Column Deadline Check - timeout, 1 consecutive error
- Weekly Architect Review (Walter) - overloaded, 1 consecutive error
- Mother Evening Routine - timeout, 1 consecutive error
- Walter Evening Summary - timeout, 1 consecutive error

### Root Cause Analysis:
- Most failures: All models failed: LLM request timed out 
- Suggests API latency/availability issues rather than harness logic
- Multiple jobs running simultaneously - potential resource contention
- Some jobs have 10+ consecutive failures (Token Usage Tracker: 11, Version Check: 12)

### Impact:
- Daily automation completely broken
- No morning/evening reports delivered to Jay
- Daily research not publishing
- Herald health monitoring offline
- Token usage tracking offline

### Immediate Actions Needed:
1. Check OpenClaw API timeout configurations
2. Review model provider status (Anthropic API)
3. Consider staggering cron schedules to reduce simultaneous load
4. Investigate if recent OpenClaw updates caused timeout regressions

items.title: Improve Windows support: docs, diagnostics, and tray companion MVP
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: Windows setup, diagnostics, and day-to-day gateway operations were harder to use and troubleshoot than the WSL2-first guidance implied.
- Why it matters: Windows users had weaker setup guidance, less actionable diagnostics, and no lightweight native tray companion for common gateway actions.
- What changed: expanded Windows docs and troubleshooting, improved install/doctor/status behavior on Windows, added a Windows tray companion MVP, and added deterministic verification paths for key Windows flows.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not fully eliminate the heavy plugin-loading path behind `doctor --non-interactive`, and targeted Vitest runs still appear host/runtime-limited on this Windows environment.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: Windows support gaps were spread across docs, install/doctor/status flows, and the absence of a lightweight native tray entry point. In addition, some Windows runtime paths were too slow or too ambiguous under non-interactive diagnostics.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Windows-specific diagnostics were not consistently bounded/actionable, and there was no deterministic tray verification path for critical user-facing behavior.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): Unknown.
- Why this regressed now: N/A
- If unknown, what was ruled out: The targeted Vitest issue was narrowed beyond the repo wrapper level; raw Vitest shows the same host-specific stall

- add a repo security scan script and fix its self-match false positive so the automation can pass on its own follow-up PRs

- make security review comment publishing automatic so the final human action stays at the merge button

items.author: romeoman

items.title: Support callback-mode dispatch for automation packs
items.content:
### Motivation
- Allow automation domains configured with mode `callback` to hand off behavior to a user-provided handler instead of running built-in automation, while preserving existing `auto` behavior and keeping `off` as a no-op.
- Ensure built-in automation only runs for packs currently in `auto` so `callback` domains do not also execute default handlers.

### Description
- Add typed callback support to plugin config via `memoryBehaviorCallbacks` and related types so each automation pack can provide a callback handler; changes in `src/plugin/config.ts`.
- Route hook registration by per-pack mode in `src/openclaw-plugin.ts`: compute `getPackBehaviorMode` per pack, derive which packs are `auto` vs `callback` vs `off`, run built-in handlers only for `auto`, and dispatch to `memoryBehaviorCallbacks` when a pack is `callback`.
- Provide safe merging of multiple `before_prompt_build` results (built-in + callbacks) and coerce callback return shapes for `message_sending` to the expected type.
- Add tests in `src/openclaw-plugin.test.ts` to verify that a `callback`-mode `session-memory` dispatches the callback instead of running built-in before-prompt automation and that callbacks are not dispatched when the pack/domain is `off`.

### Testing
- Ran `npx vitest run src/openclaw-plugin.test.ts` and the new `before_prompt_build` callback tests passed (7 tests total passed).
- Ran `npx vitest run src/plugin/config-packs.test.ts` and all config-pack tests passed (10 tests total passed).
- Ran `npm run typecheck` and it did not fully pass due to pre-existing unrelated typing errors in other test files (not introduced by this change), so type-checking remains failing on unrelated test typing issues.

------

items.title: [Bug]: openclaw agent --deliver silently drops messages (exit 0, no delivery)
items.content:
### Bug Summary

`openclaw agent --deliver --channel signal --to "+1234567890" --message "notification text"` reports success (exit 0) but the message is never delivered to the target channel. The command spawns a full LLM agent turn that processes the notification text as a user message, and the agent either responds with NO_REPLY or generates an internal response — but the actual channel delivery never happens.

### Steps to Reproduce

```bash
# This silently fails (exit 0, message never arrives on Signal):
openclaw agent --deliver --channel signal --to "+1234567890" --message "Test notification"

# This works correctly (message arrives immediately):
openclaw message send --channel signal --target "+1234567890" -m "Test notification" --json
```

### Expected Behavior

`openclaw agent --deliver` should deliver the message directly to the specified channel, similar to `openclaw message send`. It should NOT spawn an LLM turn.

### Actual Behavior

1. `agent --deliver` spawns a full LLM agent turn
2. The LLM processes the notification text as if it were a user message
3. The LLM decides there is nothing to forward (responds NO_REPLY or generates an internal response)
4. The message is never delivered to the target channel
5. Exit code is 0 (success), so the caller has no way to know delivery failed

### Impact

- **Silent notification loss**: Background automation (coding agents, cron jobs, watchers) that use `agent --deliver` to notify users silently fail. Exit 0 means no error handling triggers.
- **Tier 2 orchestration broken**: `openclaw agent --session-id <key> --message "..."` has the same issue — it injects via LLM turn instead of direct session message injection. This breaks the `--notify session:<key>` pattern for waking yielded subagents.

### Workaround

Use `openclaw message send` for channel delivery:
```bash
openclaw message send --channel signal --target "+1234567890" -m "notification" --json
```

No workaround exists for direct session injection (`agen

items.title: Add Axiom: Discord AI agent bot with Gemini free tier and extensible tool system
items.content:
Bootstraps the Axiom repo from scratch as a Discord bot backed by Google Gemini (`gemini-1.5-flash`, free tier) with a function-calling agent loop and pluggable tool system.

## Architecture

- **`src/agent.js`** — Gemini function-calling loop: sends user message → executes any requested tools → feeds results back → repeats until final text response. Maintains per-channel conversation history.
- **`src/bot.js`** — Discord client (discord.js v14). Responds to `@Axiom` mentions, prefix `axiom ...`, and DMs. Handles typing indicator, 2000-char message splitting, and `clear` to reset channel history.
- **`src/tools/`** — Registry-based tool system. Each tool exports `{ name, description, parameters (JSON Schema), execute() }`. Gemini reads the descriptions to decide when to invoke them.

## Built-in tools

| Tool | Capability |
|---|---|
| `calculator` | Safe math eval — allows `[\d\s+\-*/.%^()]` only, `^` → `**` |
| `datetime` | Current date/time in any IANA timezone |
| `remind` | In-memory timer; fires a Discord reply when it expires |
| `define` | English word definitions via free [DictionaryAPI](https://api.dictionaryapi.dev) |

## Adding a tool

Drop a file in `src/tools/` and register it in `src/tools/index.js`:

```js
export const myTool = {
  name: "my_tool",
  description: "What this does — Gemini uses this to decide when to call it.",
  parameters: {
    type: "object",
    properties: { input: { type: "string", description: "..." } },
    required: ["input"],
  },
  execute({ input }) { return `result: ${input}`; },
};
```

## Other details

- `undici` pinned to `^6.24.1` via `overrides` to address vulnerabilities bundled in discord.js v14's transitive dep.
- `DISCORD_CLIENT_ID` in `.env.example` is optional — included for future slash command registration.
- `README.md` has full setup walkthrough: Discord portal, free Gemini key, env vars, and how to extend tools.

items.title: 💡 构建 AI Coding 工作流场景库（类 Codex Use Cases）
items.content:
## 背景

OpenAI 推出了 Codex Use Cases 示例库，涵盖数据、工程、前端等六大类别。这为 AI Coding 场景标准化提供了参考范式。

飞书 lark-cli 的三层命令架构（Shortcuts / API Commands / Raw API）也值得借鉴。

## 目标

为个人工作流构建一套**结构化 AI Coding 场景库**：
1. **场景分类**：需求分析、代码生成、代码审查、重构、自动化测试、部署
2. **输入模板**：每类场景的标准提示词模板
3. **输出规范**：预期的交付物格式
4. **工具链组合**：该场景下最优的 Agent + 工具链配置

参考 lark-cli 的三层架构，设计 OpenClaw Skill 的分层调用机制。

## 子任务

- [ ] 整理 Thomas 日常高频 AI Coding 场景（≥5个）
- [ ] 为每个场景编写标准 prompt 模板
- [ ] 设计场景选择器（基于上下文自动推荐）
- [ ] 产出可复用的 OpenClaw skill snippets
- [ ] 验证 3 个典型场景的执行效果

## 预期收益

- 减少每次任务的 prompt 工程时间
- 提升 AI Coding 输出的稳定性
- 积累可迭代的工作流资产

items.title: 撰寫：learn-claude-code 研究筆記（zh-TW，可直接複用）
items.content:
## 目標
針對 repo `https://github.com/shareAI-lab/learn-claude-code` 撰寫一篇 **繁體中文（zh-TW）** 的研究筆記，放入 `content/Claude/` 底下，內容要能讓我之後快速複用。

## 產出位置
- 建議檔案：`content/Claude/learn-claude-code-guide.md`
- 若研究後覺得應放在更細的子目錄（例如 `content/Claude/Tools&Skills/`），可自行調整，但請在 issue comment 說明最終路徑。

## 核心要求
這篇不是單純摘要，而是要做成「**下次可以直接拿來用**」的筆記文章。

請至少包含以下章節：

### 1. TL;DR
- 5~10 點重點摘要
- 說清楚這個 repo 值不值得看、適合誰看、什麼情境最有用

### 2. Repo 在講什麼
- 專案定位
- 主要內容範圍
- 與 Claude Code / agent workflow / prompt engineering / tools / workspace 的關係

### 3. 分類整理（Categorised Sections）
請把內容按主題整理，不要流水帳。
至少拆成幾類（可增減）：
- Prompt / instruction patterns
- Workflow / operating habits
- Tools / integrations
- Workspace / file structure
- Safety / guardrails
- Reusable playbooks / templates

每一類都要寫：
- 這類在 repo 中講了什麼
- 為什麼重要
- 我下次可以怎麼用

### 4. 可直接複用的做法
整理成方便未來直接套用的內容，例如：
- checklist
- prompt/template
- SOP
- decision rules
- do / don't

### 5. 圖表 / 結構圖（Charts）
至少提供 2 種視覺化整理，使用 **Markdown / Mermaid** 即可，例如：
- 架構圖
- 流程圖
- decision tree
- 對照表

### 6. 與我目前工作流的連結
請加一節：**How I can use this next time**
要站在實務角度，把 learn-claude-code 的內容映射到我可能會做的事情，例如：
- 研究型 task
- coding task
- issue 撰寫
- repo 探索
- agent / sub-agent 工作流
- OpenClaw / Claude Code 類似場景

### 7. 建議保留的速查區
文末請做一個快速參考區（Quick Reference），讓我之後不用重讀整篇。

## 品質要求
- 全文使用 **繁體中文（zh-TW）**
- 結構清楚、標題命名一致
- 不是只有翻譯，要有整理、抽象、歸納
- 優先寫成「可重用知識資產」
- 若 repo 內有值得引用的範例、章節或模式，請保留連結或出處

## Definition of Done
- [ ] 在 `content/Claude/`（或合理子路徑）新增文章
- [ ] 文章為 zh-TW
- [ ] 有分類章節

- [ ] 有 `How I can use this next time` 區塊
- [ ] 有 Quick Reference

items.author: wahengchang

items.title: Feature Request: before_agent_turn plugin hook for model routing
items.content:
## Feature Request: `before_agent_turn` Plugin Hook for Model Routing

### Problem
There is no plugin hook that fires **before** the model is selected for an agent turn. This means plugins cannot dynamically route messages to different models based on message complexity, cost optimization, or other criteria.

### Current State
- `message:preprocessed` (managed hook): fires before the agent sees the message, but body mutations do not propagate to model selection
- `message_sending` (plugin hook): fires on **outbound** replies — too late to change the model
- `before_tool_call` (plugin hook): fires before tool execution, not before model dispatch
- No `before_agent_turn` or `model_select` hook exists in the current SDK

### Proposed Solution
Add a new plugin hook event — `before_agent_turn` or `model_select` — that fires after message preprocessing but **before** model dispatch. The hook should allow plugins to return:

```typescript
{
  model?: string;        // Override model for this turn only (modelOnce)
  thinkingLevel?: string; // Override thinking level for this turn
}
```

### Use Case: Smart Model Router
We built a plugin that classifies inbound message complexity via regex (zero API cost, <1ms):

| Level | Example | Target Model |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| 1 (trivial) | "hello", "yes", "thanks" | Haiku (low cost, fastest) |
| 2 (simple) | "what time is it", "check status" | Sonnet (medium cost) |  
| 3 (complex) | architecture, multi-step, coding | Opus (full power) |

The classifier works (91% test accuracy), the plugin loads and registers correctly, but there is no hook point to actually switch the model before the turn starts.

### Impact
- **Cost savings**: ~40% of messages in a typical session are trivial/simple and could use cheaper models
- **Latency**: Haiku responds 3-5x faster than Opus for simple queries
- **Token efficiency**: Smaller models use fewer output tokens for simple responses

### Implementation Notes
- Plugin is ready at `pro

items.author: rlmyfs

items.title: feat: detect syntax highlighting from shebang lines in extensionless files
items.content:
## What 
Adds shebang-based language detection to the file editor so extensionless scripts get syntax highlighting.

## Why 
Many workspace scripts don't have file extensions — they rely on shebangs like #!/usr/bin/env python3 or #!/bin/bash. Opening them in Nerve's editor showed plain unstyled text even though the language can be determined from the first line.

## How 
When a file has no extension match, getLanguageExtension() reads the first line and checks it against a small regex map of known interpreters (python, bash/sh/zsh, node/deno/bun, ruby, perl), mapping them to the corresponding CodeMirror language. Extension-based detection still takes precedence and skips shebang-based detection.

Two files changed: 
-- languageMap.ts (shebang patterns + fallback logic) 
-- FileEditor.tsx (passes file content to the resolver).

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

## Release Notes

* **New Features**
  * Improved code editor language detection by analyzing both filename and file content for more accurate syntax highlighting and language support initialization.

items.title: feat(whatsapp): add reply quoting for auto-reply messages
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: WhatsApp auto-replies are sent as standalone messages with no visual link to the triggering message. In multi-message conversations, it's unclear which reply corresponds to which inbound message.
- Why it matters: Natural WhatsApp conversations use reply quoting (swipe-to-reply) to preserve context. Telegram, Discord, and Slack already support `replyToMode` — WhatsApp is catching up.
- What changed: Thread the Baileys `quoted` message option through the WhatsApp send pipeline so auto-replies can visually quote the triggering message. Controlled by the existing `channels.whatsapp.replyToMode` config pattern (`off`/`first`/`all`), defaulting to `off`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No threading adapter registered — quoting is scoped to the auto-reply delivery path only. The shared outbound transport (message tool, follow-up delivery) does not support quoting yet. No changes to core types or other channels.

items.title: Agents: deliver media attachments from tool results
items.content:
#### Summary
Tool results that include attachments in structured `details` were only exposing `content[].text` to the reply pipeline, so channels never received the generated file path for automatic delivery. This updates tool-result handling to forward extracted media references alongside the existing text summary.

#### Repro Steps
1. In Feishu, trigger `image_generate`.
2. Observe the tool result contains a local file path under `~/.openclaw/media/tool-image-generation/...`.
3. Observe no image is automatically sent even though a manual `message action=send filePath=...` succeeds.

#### Root Cause
`handleToolExecutionEnd()` only used `extractToolResultText()`, and that helper only reads `content[].text`. The subscription layer dropped `details.media.mediaUrls` and path-style fallbacks, so outbound reply delivery never received any `mediaUrls` to send.

#### Behavior Changes
- Auto tool-result replies now forward media references from structured tool result details.
- Extraction dedupes `details.media.mediaUrls`, `details.paths`, and related path-style fallbacks.
- Added regression coverage for media-bearing tool results in non-`full` verbose mode.

#### Codebase and GitHub Search
- Reviewed issue [#57396](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/57396).
- Searched the codebase for `tool_execution_end`, `details.media`, `mediaUrls`, and tool-result reply routing.
- Verified the relevant path through `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.ts`, and `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts`.

#### Tests
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.e2e.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.waits-multiple-compaction-retries-before-resolving.e2e.test.ts -t "emits media attachments from tool result details without requiring full verbose output"`
- `pnpm test` started locally before PR creation; it is a long-running suite and was still in progress at PR creation tim
